description: Typed DSM edges between MARVEL subsystem nodes. coupling: S strong, M medium, W weak. Updated across all six subsystems for clearer interface intent and critical-path review.

edges.note: Primary heat removal path; strongly affects core temperature margins and safety envelope
Direct thermal input to convertor limits electric output and system availability
Passive decay-heat claims rely on this path; central to safety-case credibility
Thermal state feedback drives control quality, alarms, and protection logic
Indirect dependency via convertor availability and thermal derating behavior
Core physics and margins anchor shutdown logic, accident analysis, and licensing basis
Control authority and sensing fidelity determine stable operation and off-normal response
Thermal regime from core sets convertor operating envelope and efficiency
Plant-level delivery depends on upstream core reliability through conversion chain
Primary interface for usable power delivery to microgrid/data-center load
Coordinated load-following and protection needed for stable electric operation
Fault handling/protection influences safety case for integrated operations
Digital I&C evidence is a major licensing bottleneck for autonomous operation claims
Dispatch, curtailment, and protection coordination for end-user load profiles
Site acceptance, licensing conditions, and operational constraints affect deployment
